name: libkrb5-dev
title: libkrb5-dev
summary: headers and development libraries for MIT Kerberos
description:
headers and development libraries for MIT Kerberos
Kerberos is a system for authenticating users and services on a network.
Kerberos is a trusted third-party service.  That means that there is a
third party (the Kerberos server) that is trusted by all the entities on
the network (users and services, usually called "principals").
.
This is the MIT reference implementation of Kerberos V5.
.
This package contains the symlinks, headers, and development libraries
needed to compile and link programs that use the Kerberos libraries.

commands.name: krb5-config
commands.description: tool for linking against MIT Kerberos libraries

arguments.description: prints the version, vendor, prefix, and exec-prefix.

arguments.raw: --cflags  prints the compilation flags used to build the Kerberos installation.

arguments.raw: -config  tool for linking against MIT Kerberos libraries

arguments.description: prints the built-in default credentials cache location.
arguments.raw: --defccname  prints the built-in default credentials cache location.

arguments.description: prints the built-in default client (initiator) keytab location.
arguments.raw: --defcktname  prints the built-in default client (initiator) keytab location.

arguments.description: prints the built-in default keytab location.
arguments.raw: --defktname  prints the built-in default keytab location.

arguments.description: prints the prefix for executables for which the Kerberos installation was built.
arguments.raw: --exec-prefix  prints the prefix for executables for which the Kerberos installation was built.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use krb5-config because headers and development libraries for MIT Kerberos.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use krb5-config as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `krb5-config  [--help | --all | --version | --vendor | --prefix | --exec-prefix | --defccname |` as documented upstream for krb5-config.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `krb5-config  is particularly useful for compiling against a Kerberos installation that was in-` as documented upstream for krb5-config.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `shell% krb5-config --libs krb5` as documented upstream for krb5-config.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair krb5-config with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `krb5-config --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: krb5-config - tool for linking against MIT Kerberos libraries
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install krb5. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `krb5-config  [--help | --all | --version | --vendor | --prefix | --exec-prefix | --defccname |`. Further invocations from upstream: `shell% krb5-config --libs krb5`. headers and development libraries for MIT Kerberos.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: headers and development libraries for MIT Kerberos.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: krb5-config - tool for linking against MIT Kerberos libraries.
